Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util

Class FlixelString

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.FlixelString
All Implemented Interfaces:
CharSequence
public final class FlixelString extends Object implements CharSequence
Reusable mutable text buffer backed by libGDX CharArray, implementing CharSequence so it can be passed directly to libGDX text APIs (for example BitmapFont.draw(com.badlogic.gdx.graphics.g2d.Batch, java.lang.CharSequence, float, float)) without building a temporary String on each frame.

libGDX 1.14 replaced the legacy com.badlogic.gdx.utils.StringBuilder type with CharArray, which provides appenders for primitives that avoid boxing and implements CharSequence. This class wraps a single CharArray instance and exposes set(java.lang.CharSequence) overloads that clear the buffer then append new content, which matches common HUD and debug-overlay usage patterns.

Prefer passing this (as a CharSequence) into drawing and layout calls. Avoid toString() in per-frame code: it allocates a new String from the backing buffer. The same applies to string concatenation that implicitly calls toString() on this type.

Primitive set(java.lang.CharSequence) overloads and supplier-based overloads use CharArray appenders or supplier getAs* methods so primitive watches and counters do not box values before formatting.

Java 9 and later give CharSequence a default chars() method that returns an IntStream of UTF-16 code units; this class does not replace that API. Prefer clear() plus concat(java.lang.CharSequence) overloads to build text without touching CharArray at call sites. charBuffer() remains available for advanced interop with libGDX APIs that require a raw CharArray.

    @NotNull FlixelString
    concat(@Nullable Object obj)
    Appends an object the same way CharArray.append(Object) does: null becomes the literal "null", CharSequence is appended without Object.toString(), and other types use Object.toString().
    @NotNull FlixelString
    concatFloatRoundedOneDecimal(float value)
    Appends value rounded to one decimal place (tenths) using the same rules as FlixelStringUtil.appendFloatRoundedOneDecimal(CharArray, float).

    copyContentToNewString()
    Copies the current buffer into a new String.

  • Constructor Details

    • FlixelString

      public FlixelString()
      Creates an empty buffer with a default initial capacity.

    • FlixelString

      public FlixelString(int initialCapacity)
      Creates an empty buffer with the given initial capacity hint.
      Parameters:
      initialCapacity - Non-negative initial capacity for the backing CharArray.

    • FlixelString

      public FlixelString(@Nullable @Nullable CharSequence text)
      Creates a buffer whose content is a copy of text.
      Parameters:
      text - Source characters; null is treated like the literal "null".

  • Method Details

    • charBuffer

      @NotNull public @NotNull com.badlogic.gdx.utils.CharArray charBuffer()
      Returns the mutable backing CharArray. Callers must not retain references across frames if the owning FlixelString is reused or pooled, because the buffer contents change in place.
      Returns:
      The internal CharArray (never null).

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears all characters without shrinking the allocated buffer.
      See Also:
      • CharArray.clear()

    • shrinkToFit

      public void shrinkToFit()
      Shrinks the allocated buffer to the current length.
      See Also:
      • CharArray.shrink()

    • trimToSize

      public void trimToSize()
      Trims the internal storage to the current length. Suitable for teardown paths (for example FlixelText.destroy()) but not for per-frame use.

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Specified by:
      isEmpty in interface CharSequence
      Returns:
      true when the buffer contains no characters.

    • set

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelString set(@Nullable @Nullable CharSequence text)
      Replaces the entire buffer with a copy of text.
      Parameters:
      text - New content; null is treated like the literal "null".
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • set

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelString set(@Nullable @Nullable FlixelString other)
      Replaces the buffer with a copy of another FlixelString.
      Parameters:
      other - Source buffer; null is treated like the literal "null" via set(CharSequence).
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • concat

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelString concat(@NotNull @NotNull CharSequence other)
      Appends the content of other to the buffer.

      Note: If the other is a String, it will be converted to a CharSequence using String.subSequence(int, int).

      Parameters:
      other - The CharSequence to append.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • concat

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelString concat(@NotNull @NotNull FlixelString other)
      Appends the content of other to the buffer.
      Parameters:
      other - The FlixelString to append.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • concatFloatRoundedOneDecimal

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelString concatFloatRoundedOneDecimal(float value)
      Appends value rounded to one decimal place (tenths) using the same rules as FlixelStringUtil.appendFloatRoundedOneDecimal(CharArray, float). Does not clear the buffer first.
      Parameters:
      value - Value to append (non-finite values use CharArray.append(float)).
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • concat

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelString concat(@Nullable @Nullable Object obj)
      Appends an object the same way CharArray.append(Object) does: null becomes the literal "null", CharSequence is appended without Object.toString(), and other types use Object.toString().
      Parameters:
      obj - Value to append (may be null).
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setCharSequence

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelString setCharSequence(@NotNull @NotNull Supplier<? extends CharSequence> supplier)
      Replaces the buffer with the CharSequence returned by supplier. If the supplier returns null, the literal "null" is appended.

      Note: If the supplier constructs a new String on each call, allocations move to the supplier. Prefer CharSequence sources that are stable or reused when possible.

      Parameters:
      supplier - Source of the new content; must not be null.
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • setFloatRoundedOneDecimal

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelString setFloatRoundedOneDecimal(float value)
      Appends value rounded to one decimal place (tenths), using only CharArray integer appenders. This avoids Float.toString(float) and similar helpers that allocate String instances.

      The buffer is cleared before formatting.

      Parameters:
      value - Finite input; non-finite values fall back to CharArray.append(float).
      Returns:
      this for chaining.

    • copyContentToNewString

      @NotNull public @NotNull String copyContentToNewString()
      Copies the current buffer into a new String. Intended for cold paths (for example a debug forEach callback), not per-frame drawing.
      Returns:
      A new string holding the current characters.

    • length

      public int length()
      Specified by:
      length in interface CharSequence

      Specified by:
      subSequence in interface CharSequence

    • toString

      @NotNull public @NotNull String toString()

      Allocation warning: Builds a new String. Do not use on hot paths; pass this instance as a CharSequence instead.

      Specified by:
      toString in interface CharSequence
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
